about.name: Felipe Caceres - Fernandez
about.bio: Hey my name is Felipe and I'm working towards making a difference in the world through humanoid robotics. I'm an undergrad in Computational Physics at Carnegie Mellon University. Physics teaches you to reason about how the world works from first principles and computer science trains you to analyze problems with math, logic, and abstraction. I'm interested in humanoid robotics because they will have the highest capability of almost anything humans ever create and it forces you to understand different technical fields and to solve complex problems.

experience.org: Origami Robotics (Humanoid Robotics Startup, YC W26)
experience.description: Owned full-stack development of a piezoresistive FSR tactile sensing matrix for a human data-collection glove and robot hand, designing 9 FPC sensors from scratch in KiCad down to 0.2mm resolution and engineering the full stimulus-to-visualization pipeline.

experience.org: Amazon (LEO, mass satellite constellation for global internet)
experience.description: Modeling new OCV-SOC and capacity degradation data from internal cell testing to improve algorithm for battery state estimation.

projects.title: 2022 Intake Mechanism (FRC 1678)
projects.description: 4-bar linkage mechanism with powered rollers and actuation
projects.detail: Designed and built a powered four-bar linkage intake mechanism with rollers (modeled on FRC Team 1678's 2022 robot), owning the full process from CAD and prototyping through CNC and lathe fabrication, assembly, testing, and BOM. Structured master and reference sketches for modular, robust CAD across iterations, mentored five new members in assembly and CAD, and authored 11 pages of documentation.
